claims.claim: Web (browser) workflows are supported today: record a GUI workflow once, then replay it deterministically and locally.
caveats: "Supported" is scoped to the reference headless-browser backend (the v0 surface). Desktop (Windows) and Citrix are NOT supported — see their own `validating` claims below.
The full record->compile->replay e2e runs in the post-merge/nightly full suite; the fast required PR gate covers the replayer + demo units.

evidence.proves: Records the MockMed browser demo once, compiles it, and replays it under baseline + theme/move/rename drift and parameter substitution through the headless-browser Backend.
The reference browser demo app and its drift screens render deterministically (no CSS transitions), so replay is repeatable.
The deterministic replayer resolves steps, substitutes parameters, enforces postconditions and the risk gate — no model in the loop.

claims.claim: Replay is deterministic, local, and $0 — zero model calls on either the record or the replay side.
caveats: A VLM rung exists as the lowest fallback of the ladder; it is not invoked on a clean deterministic replay, and never on the record side.

evidence.proves: Baseline replay x3 resolves every step on the `template` rung with 0 heals and 0 model calls; healed bundles replay clean afterward.
The replayer is a deterministic resolution ladder; the VLM rung is a fallback, not on the default path — the core runtime is model-free.
CI-reproducible MockMed run artifact: 100/100 compiled replays at 4.9s p50 with zero model calls (field/artifact reference).

claims.claim: Effect verification against the system of record catches silent wrong writes the screen-only oracle misses (5 of 7 transactional fault classes).
caveats: Verification requires the step to DECLARE typed `effects` AND a configured EffectVerifier. Without a real system-of-record verifier, on-screen read-back is SAME-SURFACE and not independent.

evidence.proves: The REST/FHIR/document-hash verifiers confirm exactly-one write and refute duplicate / missing / partial / collateral-loss writes.
Each silent-wrong-action class is silent under the screen-only oracle and is CAUGHT once effects are declared and verified.
The FHIR effect verifier's read/confirm/refute/indeterminate contract (the live-OpenEMR smoke is a separate opt-in function in this file).
The 7-class transactional fault-model study: 5/7 classes silently mishandled by screen-only, all 5 halt through the real replayer.

claims.claim: On ambiguity the runtime halts instead of guessing, and an identity gate refuses a wrong-entity (wrong-patient) click — on ARMED steps only.
caveats: Identity verification covers ONLY armed steps. Real bundles arm a MINORITY of clicks (4 of 12 on a recent live OpenEMR bundle); an UNARMED click proceeds with no identity check whatsoever. Coverage is an auditable per-step metric, but disclosure does not close the gap.

evidence.proves: Wrong-entity / near-name sibling / generic-band / param-in-band probes: the identity check refuses to verify a mismatched target and never arms on non-discriminative bands.
Underdetermined targets surface as explicit disambiguation rather than a guessed click.
Held-out identity corpus rates (the ROC / false-accept guard behind the gate).
The identity resolution ladder (structural/template/OCR) escalation.

claims.claim: A halt is learnable: halt -> teach a correction -> promote a guarded branch, refusing underdetermined fixes.
caveats: Promotion is governed by a regression gate that blocks identity- weakening corrections; the loop refuses to guess when the fix is underdetermined.

evidence.proves: A surprise modal halts and emits a learnable trace; teaching promotes a guarded dismiss branch that replays without regression; an underdetermined correction is refused.
The `teach` CLI resolves a halt via a correction spec or a recording, refuses an underdetermined fix, and errors on a non-halted run.

claims.claim: The MockMed benchmark is CI-reproducible: compiled replay finishes ~4.9s p50 with zero model calls versus ~37.5s p50 (~$0.27/run) for the agent.
caveats: The published latency/cost figures come from a dated run artifact; the app is intentionally simple, so success rate (100/100 vs 20/20) is not the story — the standing cost/latency delta is. Agent cost is a list-price estimate.

evidence.proves: The MockMed app + drift screens are deterministic and reachable, the substrate the benchmark replays against.
The benchmark harness / report accounting that produces the numbers.
Full numbers, methodology, and caveats for the CI-reproducible MockMed comparison (dated run artifact).

claims.claim: Windows desktop workflows are being validated with design partners (not a supported surface yet): the deterministic structural (UIA) rung resolves recorded clicks by AutomationId on a real Windows app.
caveats: The live desktop e2e is OPT-IN and infra-gated (needs a Parallels Win11-ARM VM; `OAFLOW_PARALLELS_E2E=1`); it never runs on default CI. Only the mocked backend protocol runs in CI.
DataGridView rows are only partially UIA-armable (see docs/desktop/LIMITS.md). This harness does not replace third-party design-partner validation.

evidence.proves: OPT-IN live proof on a real Win11-ARM VM: record->compile->replay through WindowsBackend with the structural rung resolving every click by AutomationId (armed_coverage == 1.0).
The 4-method Backend protocol on Windows against a MOCKED WAA server (runs in CI post-merge; proves the wire, not a live desktop).
Desktop drift benchmark: the structural rung resolved 21/21 targets where visual replay alone managed 6/21.

claims.claim: Citrix / remote-display (pixel-only) is a spike being validated with design partners: the compiler's visual floor drives a real remote-display window with the structural rung unavailable, plus identity gate on pixels and halt on ambiguity.
caveats: PIXEL-ONLY: no UIA/DOM crosses to the driver. On-screen OCR read-back is SAME-SURFACE, not an independent system-of-record check.

A Parallels VM window is a Citrix ANALOG; real HDX/latency/DPI/lock- screen drift is not simulated. OPT-IN + infra-gated (`OAFLOW_CITRIX_PIXEL_E2E=1`, macOS screen-recording + accessibility); never runs on default CI, and does not replace design-partner validation.

evidence.proves: OPT-IN pixel-only proof: structural_armed_coverage == 0, replay resolves on template/ocr/geometry only, on-screen OCR read-back verifies the write, the identity gate HALTs on a look-alike patient, and render drift triggers halt-on-ambiguity.
The pixel-substrate identity probe behind the on-pixels identity gate.
The Citrix-analog methodology and the honest gaps (HDX compression, latency, DPI/credential/lock-screen drift NOT simulated).

claims.claim: On the real third-party OpenEMR public demo, compiled replay went 20/20 versus 10/10 for a computer-use agent, faster and with zero model calls.
caveats: FIELD TEST, NOT CI-reproducible: the head-to-head ran against a SHARED public demo that other users mutate and that resets daily; the sample is small (10 agent runs). Only the verifier + task-prompt units run in CI. `reproducibility: field` forbids ever labeling this `supported`.

evidence.proves: The CI-reproducible half: the note-saved verifier and the intent-not-coordinates task prompt that both benchmark arms use.
The field-test numbers, methodology, and cost caps for the 20-vs-10 head-to-head run.
The correctness-only field findings (fresh browsers, zero model calls, closed-loop scrolling).
